title_pinyin: Lín Zhèng Zhǐ Nán Yī Àn
title_en: Case Records as a Guide to Clinical Practice

significance.zh: 清代叶天士门人整理的医案巨著，按病分类，记录叶氏临证辨证、立法、遣方用药之全过程。是温病学派与清代医案学的代表性文献。
significance.en: A monumental casebook compiled by Ye Tianshi's disciples, organized by disease and preserving his complete clinical reasoning — pattern differentiation, therapeutic principle, and prescription. A defining text of the Warm Disease school and of Qing-dynasty case-based learning.

description_zh: 清代叶天士医案汇编，是温病学派临证思维的典范读本。
description_en: The compiled case records of Ye Tianshi — the canonical primer of Warm Disease school clinical reasoning.

passages.title_zh: 温热篇开宗 · 卫气营血辨证总纲
passages.title_en: Opening of the Warm-Heat Chapter: The Cardinal Principle of Wei-Qi-Ying-Xue Differentiation
passages.text: 温邪上受，首先犯肺，逆传心包。肺主气属卫，心主血属营。辨营卫气血虽与伤寒同，若论治法则与伤寒大异也。
passages.translation_zh: 温热邪气从口鼻上部而入，最先侵犯手太阴肺经；若病势逆传，则内陷手厥阴心包。肺主一身之气，司卫外；心主血脉，属营分。因此辨别卫、气、营、血四个层次，虽然与伤寒六经辨证同样是分层论病，但温病的治法（清透为主）与伤寒（辛温发表为主）迥然不同。
passages.translation_en: Warm evils are contracted from above: they first invade the Lung, and in adverse progression they sink into the Pericardium. The Lung governs qi and belongs to the protective (wei) layer; the Heart governs blood and belongs to the nutritive (ying) layer. Although differentiating ying, wei, qi, and xue parallels the layered approach of Cold Damage, the method of treatment stands in sharp contrast to that of Shang Han.

annotations.note_zh: 温邪初袭，病位在肺卫，为卫分证的定义。
annotations.note_en: The initial assault of warm evil lodges in the Lung-Wei layer — the defining moment of the Wei-fen pattern.

annotations.note_zh: 温邪不顺传气分而反陷心包，入营动血之先兆。
annotations.note_en: Warm evil bypassing the Qi layer to fall directly into the Pericardium — the harbinger of the Ying-fen stage.

annotations.phrase: 肺主气属卫，心主血属营
annotations.note_zh: 叶氏以气血统摄卫营，奠定四分层次的生理基础。
annotations.note_en: Ye Tianshi grounds the four-layer schema in the physiology of qi and blood.

annotations.note_zh: 温病忌辛温发汗，当以辛凉清透为法，与伤寒麻黄、桂枝诸法截然不同。
annotations.note_en: Warm disease forbids warm-acrid diaphoresis; its method is cool-acrid venting, wholly unlike the Mahuang/Guizhi approach of Cold Damage.

passages.title_en: Wind-Warmth Case: Failure of Lung-Wei Diffusion
passages.text: 某。风温发热，咳嗽咽痛，肢节不适，头胀，脉右寸浮数。此风温上受，肺气不宣。辛凉轻剂，桑杏汤加减。
passages.translation_zh: 某患者，感受风温之邪，发热而咳嗽、咽痛，四肢关节不舒，头胀，右寸脉浮数。此证为风温上受，肺卫失于宣发，邪在卫分。治宜辛凉轻剂，以桑杏汤加减，轻清宣透肺卫之邪。
passages.translation_en: A patient presents with wind-warmth fever, cough and sore throat, malaise of the limbs, distending headache, and a floating-rapid pulse at the right cun position. This is wind-warmth striking from above, with the Lung failing to diffuse — the evil lodged in the Wei layer. Treatment calls for a light, cool-acrid formula: Sang Xing Tang with modifications, to gently disperse and vent the evil from the Lung-Wei.

annotations.note_zh: 温邪在卫分，发热微恶风寒而咽痛。
annotations.note_en: Warm evil in the Wei layer: fever with mild aversion to wind-cold and sore throat.

annotations.note_zh: 右寸主肺，浮数示风热袭肺。
annotations.note_en: The right cun reflects the Lung; floating and rapid confirms wind-heat assailing the Lung.

annotations.note_zh: 叶氏治温病卫分证的立法，轻清宣透，忌重剂辛温。
annotations.note_en: Ye's therapeutic principle for the Wei-fen stage — light, cool, and venting; heavy warm-acrid agents are contraindicated.

annotations.note_zh: 《温病条辨》方，本案借用以宣肺润燥。
annotations.note_en: A formula from the Wen Bing Tiao Bian, applied here to diffuse the Lung and moisten dryness.

passages.title_en: Spleen-Stomach Case: Failure of the Middle Qi to Transport
passages.text: 某。脉缓无力，不思饮食，食入腹胀，大便溏薄，面色萎黄，四肢倦怠。此脾虚中气不运，清阳不升。法当甘温益气，补中益气汤主之。
passages.translation_zh: 某患者，脉象缓而无力，饮食无味，食后腹胀，大便溏泄，面色萎黄，四肢困倦乏力。此为脾气虚弱，中焦运化失职，清阳之气不能上升。当用甘温补气之法，以补中益气汤升阳益气治之。
passages.translation_en: A patient with a slow, forceless pulse, no appetite, abdominal distension after eating, loose stools, a sallow-yellow complexion, and weariness of the limbs. This is Spleen vacuity: the middle qi fails to transport and the clear yang cannot rise. The method is sweet-warm tonification of qi; Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang is the master formula.

annotations.note_zh: 脾气虚弱，运化无权，为内伤杂病之常见证。
annotations.note_en: Spleen qi vacuity with failure of transportation — a cardinal internal-damage pattern.

annotations.note_zh: 中气下陷的病机表述，李东垣升阳法所针对之证。
annotations.note_en: The pathomechanism of sinking middle qi, the target of Li Dongyuan's 'raise the yang' method.

annotations.note_zh: 缓脉为脾之本脉，无力则示虚。
annotations.note_en: A moderate pulse is the physiological pulse of the Spleen; its weakness here reveals vacuity.

annotations.note_zh: 李东垣方，甘温除热、升阳举陷的代表方。
annotations.note_en: Li Dongyuan's formula — the exemplar of sweet-warm tonification and raising the sunken yang.

passages.title_en: Liver-Wind Case: Liver Yang Transforming into Wind
passages.text: 某。头目眩晕，肢体麻木，时或抽掣，舌绛少津，脉弦细而数。此水亏木旺，肝阳化风，非外来之风也。法当柔肝熄风，育阴潜阳，不可妄投风药。
passages.translation_zh: 某患者，头晕目眩，肢体发麻，时有抽搐掣动，舌质红绛而津液不足，脉弦细而带数。此为肾水亏虚而肝木偏旺，肝阳上亢化生内风，并非外感风邪。治当柔养肝阴，平肝熄风，滋育真阴而潜降浮阳，切不可妄用祛风散邪之品。
passages.translation_en: A patient suffers dizziness and blurred vision, numbness of the limbs, intermittent twitching, a crimson tongue with scanty fluids, and a wiry-thin rapid pulse. This is Water depleted and Wood flourishing: Liver yang has transformed into internal wind — not wind from without. The method is to soften the Liver and extinguish wind, nourish yin and subdue the floating yang. Dispersing wind-medicinals must never be casually prescribed.

annotations.note_zh: 肝阳上亢的典型症候，为内风欲动之先兆。
annotations.note_en: The cardinal presentation of ascendant Liver yang — a harbinger of stirring internal wind.

annotations.note_zh: 肝肾阴亏，津液不能上承。
annotations.note_en: Liver and Kidney yin depletion — fluids cannot ascend to moisten.

annotations.note_zh: 叶氏首倡「阳化内风」之说，内风之源在肝肾阴虚。
annotations.note_en: Ye Tianshi's seminal doctrine: internal wind arises when yang transforms, rooted in Liver-Kidney yin vacuity.

annotations.note_zh: 不用辛散风药，而以酸甘咸润之品养阴潜阳。
annotations.note_en: Not with acrid wind-dispersing herbs, but with sour, sweet, and salty fluids to nourish yin and anchor yang.

passages.title_en: Cough Case: Dryness Injuring Lung Yin
passages.text: 某。秋燥伤肺，干咳无痰，咽喉不利，鼻燥唇干，脉右寸细数。此燥邪化火，劫烁肺津。宜辛凉甘润，桑杏汤加沙参、麦冬之属。
passages.translation_zh: 某患者，秋令燥邪伤肺，干咳而无痰，咽喉不利，鼻干唇燥，右寸脉细数。此为温燥化火，耗伤肺中津液，肺失清润。治宜辛凉甘润，以桑杏汤加沙参、麦冬等养阴润肺之品。
passages.translation_en: A patient in autumn, dryness injuring the Lung: dry cough without phlegm, discomfort of the throat, dry nose and parched lips, a thin-rapid pulse at the right cun. This is dryness transforming into fire, scorching the Lung fluids. The method is cool-acrid and sweet-moistening — Sang Xing Tang supplemented with Sha Shen and Mai Dong.

annotations.note_zh: 温燥伤阴，肺津受灼，发为干咳无痰。
annotations.note_en: Warm dryness injuring yin — Lung fluids scorched, producing dry cough without phlegm.

annotations.note_zh: 轻宣燥邪，凉润肺金。
annotations.note_en: Lightly disperses dryness and cool-moistens the Metal organ.

annotations.note_zh: 甘凉养阴，清肺生津。
annotations.note_en: Sweet and cool, nourishes yin, clears the Lung, and engenders fluids.

annotations.note_zh: 甘寒润肺，清心除烦。
annotations.note_en: Sweet and cold — moistens the Lung and clears Heart vexation.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Binhu's Sphygmology

significance.zh: 明代李时珍所著脉学专书，以歌诀体系统论述二十七脉（后世扩为二十八脉）的脉象、主病与鉴别要点。语言精炼，便于记诵，是历代学脉者必读经典。
significance.en: Li Shizhen's Ming-dynasty pulse monograph, presenting the 27 (later 28) classical pulses through memorable verse, covering pulse image, principal indications, and points of differentiation. Concise and mnemonic, it has remained the standard primer for pulse study across centuries.

description_zh: 明代李时珍著，以歌诀体系统论述二十八脉，是脉学入门与临证参考的权威文献。
description_en: Li Shizhen's Ming-dynasty verse primer on the 28 classical pulses, the authoritative reference for sphygmology in both study and clinical practice.

passages.title_en: The Floating Pulse: Verse on Its Form
passages.text: 浮脉惟从肉上行，如循榆荚似毛轻。三秋得令知无恙，久病逢之却可惊。
passages.translation_zh: 浮脉的特征是脉气行于肌肉之上，轻轻按之即得，指下如同抚循榆荚，又像羽毛般轻飘。秋令三月肺金当令，见浮脉乃四时正脉，不足为病；但久病虚损之人忽现浮脉，则是正气浮越、根气欲脱之象，最为可惊。
passages.translation_en: The Floating pulse travels only upon the flesh; under the finger it feels like brushing elm seeds, light as down. In the three months of autumn it is the seasonal pulse — no cause for alarm. Yet when one chronically ill meets it, the root qi is deserting — and that is truly to be feared.

annotations.note_zh: 二十八脉之首，轻取即得，重按稍减，主表证。
annotations.note_en: The first of the twenty-eight pulses — felt on light touch and diminished on pressure; it governs exterior patterns.

annotations.note_zh: 肺应秋，脉应浮，为四时平脉。
annotations.note_en: The Lung corresponds to autumn; its proper pulse is floating — the physiological pulse of the season.

annotations.note_zh: 久病本当沉细，忽浮为虚阳外浮，根气将脱之候。
annotations.note_en: Chronic disease should present sinking and thin pulses; a sudden floating pulse betrays deserting yang — a dire sign.

passages.title_en: The Sinking Pulse: Verse on Its Form
passages.text: 水行润下脉来沉，筋骨之间软滑匀。女子寸兮男子尺，四时如此号为平。
passages.translation_zh: 沉脉如水性润下，脉气潜行于筋骨之间，重按乃得，指下柔软、滑利、均匀。女子两寸脉、男子两尺脉本来偏沉，若四季皆如此而无他异，即为平人之常脉，并非病候。
passages.translation_en: Like water that flows downward, the Sinking pulse comes deep; between sinew and bone it is soft, slippery, and even. In women at the cun and in men at the chi it naturally sinks — and if it holds so through the four seasons, it is called the balanced pulse of the healthy.

annotations.note_zh: 与浮脉相对，重按乃得，主里证。
annotations.note_en: The counterpart of the Floating pulse — obtained on deep pressure; it governs interior patterns.

annotations.note_zh: 李时珍指出生理性沉脉的部位差异，不可一概作病论。
annotations.note_en: Li Shizhen notes physiological sinking at sex-specific positions — it should not be taken as pathology.

annotations.note_zh: 平脉即无病之常脉，区别于病理性沉脉。
annotations.note_en: The 'balanced pulse' — the physiological baseline — distinguished from the pathological sinking pulse.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Compendium of Materia Medica

significance.zh: 中国药学史上最伟大的著作之一。全书五十二卷，载药1892种，方剂11096首，附药图1109幅。以纲目分类法重新编排本草，对后世本草学影响深远。
significance.en: One of the greatest works in the history of Chinese pharmacology. Spanning 52 volumes, it documents 1,892 medicinal substances, 11,096 formulas, and 1,109 illustrations. Its taxonomic classification system reorganized materia medica and profoundly influenced subsequent pharmacological scholarship.

description_zh: 《本草纲目》为明代医药学家李时珍历时二十七年编成之本草巨著。全书以「纲」「目」二级分类，将药物分为十六部六十类，首创自然分类法。书中不仅汇集历代本草精华，更以亲身考察、实验验证为据，纠正前人诸多谬误。
description_en: The Compendium of Materia Medica (Bencao Gangmu) is a monumental pharmacological work compiled by the Ming-dynasty physician-naturalist Li Shizhen over twenty-seven years. It organizes medicinal substances into 16 divisions and 60 classes using a pioneering two-level taxonomic system. The work synthesizes centuries of pharmacological knowledge while correcting numerous errors through firsthand investigation and empirical verification.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Xiǎo Ér Yào Zhèng Zhí Jué
title_en: Key to Therapeutics of Children's Diseases

significance.zh: 中国现存最早的儿科专著之一。钱乙在书中创立了小儿五脏辨证体系，并化裁古方为六味地黄丸等经典方剂，对后世儿科学和方剂学影响深远。
significance.en: One of the earliest extant Chinese monographs on pediatrics. Qian Yi established a Five-Organ pattern differentiation system for children and adapted classical formulas — most notably Liu Wei Di Huang Wan — profoundly influencing later pediatric and formulary traditions.

description_zh: 宋代儿科名家钱乙的临证经验，由其弟子阎孝忠整理而成。全书论述小儿生理病理特点，以五脏为纲辨证施治，载方百余首，为中医儿科学的奠基之作。
description_en: A record of the clinical experience of the Song-dynasty pediatric master Qian Yi, compiled by his disciple Yan Xiaozhong. The text discusses the physiological and pathological characteristics of children, organizes diagnosis and treatment around the Five Organs, and contains over one hundred formulas. It is regarded as the founding work of Chinese pediatric medicine.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Bèi Jí Qiān Jīn Yào Fāng
title_en: Essential Formulas Worth a Thousand Gold for Emergencies

significance.zh: 唐代孙思邈所著的综合性医学巨著，共三十卷。书中广收前代经方并加入作者临证经验，内容涵盖内、外、妇、儿、五官、针灸、食治、养生等几乎全部医学门类，是唐以前医学成就的集大成者，对后世方剂学与临床医学影响极为深远。
significance.en: A comprehensive thirty-volume medical work by the Tang-dynasty physician Sun Simiao. It gathers classical formulas from earlier periods and integrates the author's own clinical experience, covering internal, external, gynecological, pediatric, ophthalmological, acupuncture, dietary, and nourishing-life disciplines. It represents the culmination of pre-Tang medical knowledge and exerted a profound influence on subsequent formulary and clinical traditions.

description_zh: 唐代孙思邈撰，共三十卷，载方逾五千首。全书以「人命至重，有贵千金」为立论根本，强调医德与临床实用并重，为中国现存最早的医学百科全书之一。
description_en: Compiled by the Tang-dynasty physician Sun Simiao in thirty volumes, the work records more than five thousand formulas. Its guiding principle — that a human life is worth more than a thousand pieces of gold — places medical ethics on equal footing with clinical utility. It stands as one of the earliest extant encyclopedic medical compendia in Chinese history.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Dilations on the Materia Medica

significance.zh: 北宋寇宗奭所撰，是宋代本草学承前启后的重要著作。书中在《嘉祐本草》、《图经本草》的基础上对药性、形态、炮制、鉴别加以辨析考订，影响后世诸家本草，尤为金元诸家所重。
significance.en: A pivotal Northern Song materia medica by Kou Zongshi that builds upon the Jiayou Bencao and Tujing Bencao. By reexamining drug nature, morphology, processing, and identification, the work provided critical corrections and influenced later herbal scholarship, particularly valued by the great Jin-Yuan physicians.

description_zh: 北宋寇宗奭撰，全书二十卷，对四百余种药物的性味、功效、鉴别与使用经验加以阐发和辨正，被金元医家张元素、李东垣、朱丹溪等多所引用。
description_en: A twenty-volume work by the Northern Song pharmacologist Kou Zongshi, elaborating and correcting the properties, actions, identification, and clinical use of more than four hundred medicinal substances. It was widely cited by Jin-Yuan masters such as Zhang Yuansu, Li Dongyuan, and Zhu Danxi, and shaped later pharmacological thought.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Chóng Dìng Tōng Sú Shāng Hán Lùn
title_en: Revised Popular Treatise on Cold Damage

author.zh: 俞根初 原著；何廉臣 重订

significance.zh: 清末绍派伤寒代表医家俞根初原著《通俗伤寒论》，经何廉臣重订、增注、刊行，将伤寒与温病学说熔于一炉，并结合江南地区多湿多温的实际临证经验，是近代南方伤寒学派的重要代表作。
significance.en: He Lianchen's revision, annotation, and publication of Yu Genchu's Tongsu Shanghan Lun, a defining work of the Shaoxing school of Cold Damage. The text fuses Cold Damage and Warm Disease doctrines and adapts them to the damp, warm climate of the Jiangnan region, making it a principal expression of modern southern Cold Damage scholarship.

description_zh: 本书在俞根初《通俗伤寒论》的基础上，由清末民初绍兴名医何廉臣补订增注而成。书中以伤寒六经为纲，融合温病卫气营血与三焦辨证，议论六淫兼夹、湿温秋燥尤详，体例切合江南临证。
description_en: This edition builds on Yu Genchu's Tongsu Shanghan Lun with extensive revisions and commentary by the late-Qing/early-Republican Shaoxing physician He Lianchen. Organized around the Six Channels of Cold Damage, the work integrates Warm Disease doctrines of the Four Levels and Three Burners, with particular attention to mixed contractions, Damp-Warmth, and autumn Dryness as encountered in Jiangnan clinical practice.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: These Things Are Hard to Know

significance.zh: 元代易水学派医家王好古所著，记录其师张元素、李东垣论医之语及自己的心得。书中阐发脏腑辨证、六经与脏腑病机之关系、用药归经之说，是易水学派理论的重要代表作。
significance.en: A Yuan-dynasty work by Wang Haogu recording the medical teachings of his masters Zhang Yuansu and Li Dongyuan together with his own reflections. It develops Zang-Fu pattern differentiation, the relationship between the Six Channels and the Zang-Fu, and the theory of channel-entry of medicinals — representing key doctrines of the Yishui current.

description_zh: 元代王好古撰，名「此事难知」以示医理精微难尽。全书上下两卷，问答体裁，辑录师说与己见，所论脏腑用药、六经归属，深得易水学派心法。
description_en: Composed in the Yuan dynasty by Wang Haogu, the title — literally 'These Things Are Hard to Know' — acknowledges the subtlety of medical doctrine. Arranged in two volumes in question-and-answer form, it assembles the teachings of his masters together with his own insights, expressing the core clinical reasoning of the Yishui school.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Teachings of Master Danxi

author.zh: 朱震亨（朱丹溪）原著；门人整理
author.en: Zhu Zhenheng (Zhu Danxi); compiled by his disciples

significance.zh: 元代养阴学派创始人朱丹溪的临证心得，由其门人整理而成。书中首倡「阳常有余，阴常不足」之说，重视相火、滋阴降火，开明清养阴理论之先河，为金元四大家思想的重要代表。
significance.en: A compilation of the clinical teachings of Zhu Danxi, founder of the Yin-Nourishing current, put together by his disciples. Articulating the famous thesis that 'yang is constantly in surplus, yin constantly insufficient,' the work highlights ministerial fire and the need to enrich yin and clear fire — an outlook that shaped Ming and Qing medical thinking and remains central among the Four Masters of the Jin-Yuan.

description_zh: 元代朱丹溪临证思想的集大成作，由其弟子整理成书。全书按病分类，论治外感、内伤、妇、儿等病证，尤以阐发滋阴降火法为特色，影响明清医学甚深。
description_en: The definitive compilation of Zhu Danxi's clinical doctrine, assembled by his disciples after his death. Arranged by disease, the text treats externally contracted and internal damage conditions as well as gynecological and pediatric complaints, distinguished throughout by its emphasis on enriching yin and clearing fire — a method that profoundly shaped Ming and Qing medicine.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Fu Qingzhu's Gynecology

significance.zh: 清代傅山所遗妇科经验，身后由后人辑刊。书中辨证精当、立方简要、疗效显著，尤以调理肝脾肾治妇人经带胎产为特色，是清代妇科学的代表性著作之一。
significance.en: A gynecological manuscript by the early-Qing physician Fu Shan, printed posthumously. Prized for its precise pattern differentiation, concise prescriptions, and strong clinical results — particularly through the regulation of Liver, Spleen, and Kidney in menstrual, leucorrheal, gestational, and postpartum disorders — it is regarded as one of the most influential Qing-dynasty works on women's medicine.

description_zh: 清代傅山所著妇科专书，身后始有刻本传世。全书以经、带、胎、产、崩、漏诸门为纲，每证一方，方多以调肝、补脾、益肾立法，为后世临床妇科所宗。
description_en: A Qing-dynasty monograph on women's medicine by Fu Shan, first printed long after his death. Organized under the headings of menstruation, leucorrhea, pregnancy, childbirth, flooding, and spotting, each pattern is paired with a single formula. The prescriptions characteristically soothe the Liver, tonify the Spleen, and supplement the Kidney, forming a clinical model widely followed in later gynecology.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Fù Rén Dà Quán Liáng Fāng
title_en: Complete Compendium of Fine Formulas for Women

significance.zh: 南宋陈自明所著，是中国现存较早且体系完整的妇科学专著。全书二十四卷，分八门，论述调经、众疾、求嗣、胎教、妊娠、坐月、产难、产后，奠定了后世中医妇科学的基本格局。
significance.en: A Southern Song gynecological compendium by Chen Ziming and one of the earliest systematically organized works on women's medicine in the Chinese tradition. Its twenty-four volumes are arranged in eight divisions — menstruation, miscellaneous disorders, conception, fetal education, pregnancy, confinement, difficult labor, and postpartum — establishing the structural framework followed by later TCM gynecology.

description_zh: 南宋陈自明撰，成书于淳祐七年，是现存较早的综合性妇产科专书。全书分八门二百六十余论，每论后附方，广集宋以前妇科经验，为中医妇科学之源流代表作。
description_en: Written by the Southern Song physician Chen Ziming and completed in 1237, this is one of the earliest extant comprehensive works on obstetrics and gynecology. Organized into eight divisions and more than 260 discussions — each followed by formulas — it synthesizes gynecological knowledge from before the Song and serves as a foundational source for the TCM gynecological tradition.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Formulas to Aid the Living

significance.zh: 南宋严用和积其四十年临证经验而成的方书。书中立论重视脾肾、擅用温补，为后世肾气丸类方的发展提供了重要源流，如济生肾气丸、归脾汤等皆载于此书。
significance.en: A Southern Song formulary distilled from Yan Yonghe's forty years of clinical practice. Its doctrinal emphasis on the Spleen and Kidney and on warming-supplementing strategies provided a crucial source for later developments of the Kidney Qi line — including Jisheng Shenqi Wan and Gui Pi Tang, both first recorded here.

description_zh: 南宋严用和著，成书于宝祐元年。全书十卷，载方四百余首，内容兼及内、外、妇、儿各科。其温补脾肾、调和气血之论，对金元以后诸家颇有启发。
description_en: A ten-volume Southern Song formulary by Yan Yonghe, completed in 1253 and containing over four hundred formulas spanning internal, external, gynecological, and pediatric medicine. Its doctrines on warming-supplementing the Spleen and Kidney and regulating qi and blood influenced numerous physicians of the Jin-Yuan and later eras.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Jiào Zhù Fù Rén Liáng Fāng
title_en: Annotated Edition of the Fine Formulas for Women

author.zh: 薛己 校注；陈自明 原著

significance.zh: 明代薛己在陈自明《妇人大全良方》基础上删繁补缺、加以校注而成。薛己重视脾胃与命门，临证多用八珍、逍遥、归脾、六味、八味诸方，使本书兼具宋代妇科经典与明代温补学派特色。
significance.en: A Ming edition in which Xue Ji revises and annotates Chen Ziming's Fine Formulas for Women. Xue Ji's emphasis on the Spleen-Stomach and the Gate of Vitality — expressed through his frequent use of Ba Zhen Tang, Xiao Yao San, Gui Pi Tang, Liu Wei Di Huang Wan, and Ba Wei Di Huang Wan — gives this edition both the authority of the Song gynecological classic and the distinctive flavor of the Ming warming-supplementing current.

description_zh: 明薛己校注，成书于嘉靖年间。本书在南宋陈自明原书基础上删订、补入薛氏治验，并以温补法融入妇科调治，为后世妇科常用参考。
description_en: Xue Ji's Ming-dynasty annotated edition of Chen Ziming's Southern Song gynecological classic, produced during the Jiajing reign. Xue Ji trims and revises the original text while integrating his own clinical records and characteristic warming-supplementing approach, producing a reference frequently consulted in later gynecological practice.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: The Complete Works of Jingyue

significance.zh: 明代张景岳集毕生学术与临证经验而成的综合性医学巨著，共六十四卷。书中系统阐发「阳非有余，阴本不足」的理论，倡导温补真阴真阳，是明代温补学派的代表著作，并对《内经》、脉法、方药、各科病证作系统论述。
significance.en: A comprehensive sixty-four-volume Ming work by Zhang Jingyue gathering the fruits of his lifelong scholarship and practice. Systematically developing the thesis that 'yang is not in surplus, yin is fundamentally insufficient,' it champions warming and supplementing true yin and true yang — the hallmark doctrine of the Ming warming-supplementing current — and provides comprehensive commentary on the Neijing, pulse diagnosis, formulas, and each clinical specialty.

description_zh: 明代张景岳撰，凡六十四卷，涵盖医论、脉法、伤寒、杂证、妇人、小儿、外科、本草、新方八阵、古方八阵等。其温补学说与辨证思路，对明清以后临床医学影响深远。
description_en: A sixty-four-volume Ming compendium by Zhang Jingyue, covering medical doctrine, pulse diagnosis, Cold Damage, miscellaneous disorders, gynecology, pediatrics, external medicine, materia medica, and the Eight Arrays of new and ancient formulas. Its warming-supplementing theory and clinical reasoning exerted a lasting influence on later Ming and Qing medicine.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Secret Treasury of the Orchid Chamber

significance.zh: 金代补土派宗师李东垣晚年所撰，与《脾胃论》、《内外伤辨惑论》同为其学术三部曲之一。书中详述内伤脾胃致病之机与辨治，载补中益气汤等众多传世名方。
significance.en: A late work by the Jin-dynasty master Li Dongyuan, founder of the Spleen-Earth-Supplementing current, and one of the three principal expressions of his doctrine alongside the Piwei Lun and the Neiwai Shangbian Huolun. It explores the pathogenesis and treatment of internal damage to the Spleen and Stomach and records enduring formulas including Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang.

description_zh: 金代李东垣撰，共三卷。书以脾胃内伤立论，分饮食劳倦、妇人杂病、疮疡等门，每门下详陈病机、辨证与方药，是补土派临床经验的集中体现。
description_en: A three-volume Jin-dynasty work by Li Dongyuan, grounded in his doctrine of internal damage to the Spleen and Stomach. Arranged into sections on dietary and taxation damage, women's miscellaneous disorders, sores and lesions, and more, each chapter details pathogenesis, pattern differentiation, and formulas — a concentrated record of the clinical legacy of the Spleen-Earth-Supplementing current.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Excerpts on Internal Medicine

significance.zh: 明代薛己所著，以内科病证为主的验案选录，是现存较早以「内科」命名、以病案体裁为主的著作之一。书中体现薛氏重视脾胃、肾命门，善用温补方药的学术特点。
significance.en: A Ming-dynasty casebook by Xue Ji focused on internal-medicine disorders — one of the earliest extant works to use 'internal medicine' in its title and to foreground the case-record genre. It expresses Xue Ji's characteristic emphasis on the Spleen-Stomach and the Kidney Gate of Vitality, treated through warming-supplementing formulas.

description_zh: 明代薛己撰，上下二卷。书中以作者亲历的内科病案为主，辨证论治紧扣脾胃与肾命，常用八珍、归脾、六味、八味等方，反映明代温补学派的临床面貌。
description_en: A two-volume Ming case-record work by Xue Ji. Composed chiefly of internal-medicine cases drawn from his own practice, it ties pattern differentiation and treatment tightly to the Spleen-Stomach and the Kidney Gate of Vitality, frequently employing Ba Zhen Tang, Gui Pi Tang, Liu Wei Di Huang Wan, and Ba Wei Di Huang Wan — a characteristic portrait of Ming warming-supplementing practice.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Formulas of Universal Benefit from My Own Practice

significance.zh: 南宋伤寒大家许叔微所著，是中国早期医学医案体著作之一。书中记录作者亲见亲治之验案三百余首，每案附方并加按语，以实录形式保存了宋代经方临证运用的宝贵经验。
significance.en: A Southern Song work by the Cold Damage master Xu Shuwei, and one of the earliest Chinese texts in the case-record genre. It preserves more than three hundred cases drawn from the author's own practice, each paired with a formula and commentary — an unvarnished record of how Song-era physicians deployed the classical formulas at the bedside.

description_zh: 南宋许叔微撰，共十卷。本书以医案与方剂相结合的体例记述作者临证心得，内容涵盖内、外、妇、儿各科，为后世医案学发展之源流。
description_en: A ten-volume Southern Song work by Xu Shuwei, combining clinical case records with their associated formulas. Its cases span internal, external, gynecological, and pediatric medicine, and the work is recognized as a source for the later development of the Chinese medical case-record tradition.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Six Texts on Cold Damage

significance.zh: 明代陶华所辑伤寒学著作合集，包括《琐言》、《家秘》、《杀车槌法》、《一提金》、《截江网》、《明理续论》六种，对仲景学说加以阐释、归纳与简化，使伤寒辨证更便于临证掌握。
significance.en: A Ming collection by Tao Hua gathering six of his own works on Cold Damage — the Suoyan, Jiami, Shachechui Fa, Yitijin, Jiejiangwang, and Mingli Xulun. Together they interpret, systematize, and simplify Zhang Zhongjing's doctrines, making Cold Damage pattern differentiation more accessible in daily clinical practice.

description_zh: 明陶华所著伤寒诸论合编，总称《伤寒六书》。书中以简明易学的方式归纳伤寒六经辨证，提出诸多临证辨治口诀，对明以后伤寒普及影响深远。
description_en: A Ming-dynasty collection of Tao Hua's six treatises on Cold Damage, published together as the Shanghan Liushu. Through accessible, practical expositions of Six-Channel pattern differentiation and numerous clinical mnemonics, the work strongly influenced the popularization of Cold Damage doctrine after the Ming.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Numerous Marvelous Formulas for Nourishing Life

significance.zh: 明代张时彻所辑方书，共十一卷，广收历代与时人验方，按病证分门编次，兼及内、外、妇、儿诸科，为明代民间应用方剂的重要汇编。
significance.en: An eleven-volume Ming formulary compiled by Zhang Shiche that gathers tested prescriptions from past eras and contemporaneous physicians. Organized by disease category and spanning internal, external, gynecological, and pediatric medicine, it is an important compilation of formulas in common use during the Ming.

description_zh: 明张时彻辑，成书于嘉靖年间。全书分诸风、诸气、痰饮、伤寒、杂证、妇人、小儿、外科、五官等门，每门辑录众方，是一部民间实用性强的综合方书。
description_en: Compiled by Zhang Shiche during the Jiajing reign of the Ming dynasty, the work is organized under headings such as wind disorders, qi disorders, phlegm-rheum, Cold Damage, miscellaneous disorders, women's medicine, pediatrics, external medicine, and the orifices. Each section assembles numerous formulas drawn from practical use, giving the book a strong empirical and popular character.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Sù Wèn Bìng Jī Qì Yí Bǎo Mìng Jí
title_en: Collection on Suwen Disease Mechanisms and Seasonal Qi for the Preservation of Life

significance.zh: 金代寒凉学派宗师刘完素所著，是其学术思想的代表作之一。书中阐发《素问》病机十九条，结合运气学说，提出「六气皆从火化」、「五志过极皆为热」的重要观点，开后世火热论先河，并为温病学说奠定了理论基础。
significance.en: A signature work of the Jin-dynasty master Liu Wansu, founder of the Cold-Cool current. Elaborating the Nineteen Disease Mechanisms of the Suwen in light of the Five Movements and Six Qi, it advances the celebrated theses that 'the six qi all transform through fire' and 'excesses of the five emotions all become heat' — ideas that anchored the later Fire-Heat doctrine and laid theoretical foundations for Warm Disease scholarship.

description_zh: 金代刘完素撰，共三卷。本书在阐发《素问》病机理论的基础上，结合临证提出以清热泻火为主的治疗思路，载方亦多以凉润为法，是寒凉学派的经典文献。
description_en: A three-volume Jin-dynasty work by Liu Wansu. Building on the disease-mechanism theory of the Suwen, it articulates a clinical orientation centered on clearing heat and draining fire, with its formulas correspondingly favoring cooling and moistening approaches — a foundational document of the Cold-Cool current.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Popular Treatise on Cold Damage

significance.zh: 清代绍兴医家俞根初所著，绍派伤寒学的奠基之作。书中将仲景伤寒与温病学说融会贯通，结合江南气候与临证经验，形成兼收并蓄、以湿温为特色的学术体系，对近代伤寒学影响深远。
significance.en: A foundational work of the Shaoxing school of Cold Damage by the late-Qing physician Yu Genchu. Fusing Zhang Zhongjing's Cold Damage doctrine with Warm Disease scholarship and adapting both to the climate and clinical realities of Jiangnan, it produced a broad, integrative tradition with a distinctive focus on Damp-Warmth that deeply shaped modern Cold Damage studies.

description_zh: 清末俞根初撰，取仲景之法而融汇温热诸家，结合江南多湿多温的气候特点，立论兼收并蓄，用药灵活轻灵。本书原稿几经流传，清末民初由何廉臣重订刊行，即后世《重订通俗伤寒论》之所本。
description_en: A late-Qing work by Yu Genchu that adopts Zhang Zhongjing's methods while integrating Warm-Heat scholarship and adapting to the damp, warm climate of Jiangnan. Its doctrine is broadly integrative, its prescribing light and flexible. After circulating in manuscript, the text was revised and printed by He Lianchen in the late Qing and early Republic, providing the foundation for the later Chongding Tongsu Shanghan Lun.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Wài Kē Zhèng Zhì Quán Shēng Jí
title_en: Complete Life-Preserving Collection on the Patterns and Treatment of External Medicine

significance.zh: 清代王维德所著，是清代外科全生派的代表作。书中创立「阴阳辨证」为外科总纲，主张痈阳疽阴，治阳证用清凉、治阴证用温通，传世名方阳和汤、小金丹、犀黄丸、醒消丸皆载于此书。
significance.en: A Qing-dynasty work by Wang Weide and the defining text of the Quansheng (Life-Preserving) current of external medicine. Establishing a yin-yang pattern framework as the core of surgical diagnosis — with 'yong' sores as yang and 'ju' sores as yin, treated respectively by cooling and by warming-unblocking methods — it transmits the celebrated formulas Yang He Tang, Xiao Jin Dan, Xi Huang Wan, and Xing Xiao Wan.

description_zh: 清代王洪绪所撰外科专书，三卷本流传最广。书中以阴阳辨证为纲论治外科痈疽疮疡，强调内消、内托法，反对滥用刀针，载方简要而疗效显著，深受后世外科医家推崇。
description_en: A Qing-dynasty monograph on external medicine by Wang Hongxu, most widely circulated in the three-volume edition. It treats abscesses, carbuncles, and sores through yin-yang pattern differentiation, emphasizes internal dissipation and internal supporting methods, and cautions against excessive use of the knife. Its concise but highly effective formulas made it one of the most admired surgical references of the Qing.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Orthodox Lineage of External Medicine

significance.zh: 明代陈实功所著，是中医外科学最重要的经典之一。全书四卷，系统论述一百余种外科病证的病因、病机、辨证、治法与方药，内治与外治并重，内消、内托、艾火、刀针等法详备，对后世外科学影响深远。
significance.en: A Ming-dynasty work by Chen Shigong and one of the most important classics of external medicine in the Chinese tradition. Its four volumes systematically address the etiology, pathogenesis, pattern differentiation, treatment method, and formulas for more than one hundred surgical conditions, giving equal weight to internal and external therapies and detailing internal dissipation, internal supporting, moxibustion, and surgical techniques — a profound influence on later external medicine.

description_zh: 明代陈实功撰，凡四卷。书中广论痈疽、疔疮、瘰疬、瘿瘤、乳痈、肛门病、跌打损伤等外科病证，强调辨证施治、内外并重，载方与外治法极为丰富，被誉为明代外科学之集大成者。
description_en: A four-volume Ming work by Chen Shigong covering abscesses, carbuncles, furuncles, scrofula, goiter, tumors, mammary abscess, anorectal disorders, and traumatic injuries. Stressing pattern-based treatment and the unity of internal and external therapies, and preserving an unusually rich store of formulas and topical techniques, it is regarded as the synthesis of Ming surgical learning.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: The Warp and Weft of Warm-Heat Diseases

significance.zh: 清代温病大家王孟英所辑编，是温病学的重要综合文献。书中以《内经》、仲景论温为「经」，以叶天士、薛生白、陈平伯、余师愚诸家温病专论为「纬」，并加以按语评注，使温病学说系统条贯。
significance.en: A key synthesis of Warm Disease scholarship compiled by the Qing master Wang Mengying. It takes the Warm-disease passages of the Neijing and Zhang Zhongjing as the warp, the monographs of Ye Tianshi, Xue Shengbai, Chen Pingbo, and Yu Shiyu as the weft, and adds the compiler's own commentary — drawing the full body of Warm Disease doctrine into a coherent whole.

description_zh: 清王孟英撰于咸丰二年。全书五卷，集《内经》、仲景、叶天士、薛生白、陈平伯、余师愚温热之论，加以按语评析，并载治法与方药，为清代温病学的系统总汇。
description_en: A five-volume Qing work by Wang Mengying, completed in 1852. It assembles the Warm-disease writings of the Neijing, Zhang Zhongjing, Ye Tianshi, Xue Shengbai, Chen Pingbo, and Yu Shiyu, supplies commentary and analysis, and records treatment methods and formulas — standing as the most comprehensive systematization of Qing Warm Disease doctrine.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Sequel to the Classified Case Records of Famous Physicians

significance.zh: 清代魏之琇辑，是继江瓘《名医类案》之后最重要的中医医案总汇。全书三十六卷，按病分门，收录宋元明清诸家名医验案，材料宏富，保存了大量临床实例，为研究中医辨证论治的重要文献。
significance.en: Compiled by the Qing-dynasty scholar Wei Zhixiu as the successor to Jiang Guan's Mingyi Lei'an, this is among the most substantial Chinese case-record compendia. Its thirty-six volumes, arranged by disease, preserve cases from renowned Song, Yuan, Ming, and Qing physicians — a vast clinical archive indispensable for the study of TCM pattern differentiation.

description_zh: 清魏之琇纂辑，乾隆中叶刊行。全书广采宋以降众家名医验案，按病分门类编，每案多加按语，为医案学重要典籍之一。
description_en: Compiled by the Qing-dynasty scholar Wei Zhixiu and first printed in the mid-Qianlong era. Gathering case records from distinguished physicians from the Song onward, arranged by disease category and frequently accompanied by commentary, it is one of the central reference works of the Chinese medical case-record tradition.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Investigations of Medical Formulas

significance.zh: 明代吴昆所著，是中国现存较早的方剂学专论性著作之一，开方剂学「以理论方」之风。全书选录古今常用方剂七百余首，按病证分二十类，每方详考组方之理、药物作用及加减变化。
significance.en: A Ming-dynasty work by Wu Kun and one of the earliest Chinese monographs devoted specifically to the theoretical analysis of formulas. It gathers more than seven hundred commonly used ancient and contemporary prescriptions, arranges them into twenty disease categories, and examines for each the logic of composition, the role of individual herbs, and the rationale for modifications — pioneering the discipline of formula theory.

description_zh: 明吴昆撰，成书于万历十二年。全书六卷，按病类编次方剂七百余首，每方先列主治，次解方义，剖析组方配伍与君臣佐使，是中医方剂学的重要理论著作。
description_en: A six-volume Ming work by Wu Kun, completed in 1584. It arranges more than seven hundred formulas by disease category, presenting for each the indications, the meaning of the formula, and a detailed analysis of composition through the sovereign-minister-assistant-courier framework — a foundational theoretical treatise of Chinese formula studies.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Yī Xué Zhōng Zhōng Cān Xī Lù
title_en: Records of Medicine with Reference to the West While Holding Fast to the Chinese

significance.zh: 近代张锡纯毕生临证、讲学与中西医参证之心得，是近代中医最具代表性的综合著作之一。全书以中医为本，参酌西说，创立大量临证验方（如升陷汤、镇肝熄风汤、参赭镇气汤等），为近代中西医汇通派的奠基之作。
significance.en: The life's work of the modern physician Zhang Xichun — a synthesis of clinical experience, teaching, and comparative East-West medical reasoning. Grounded firmly in Chinese medicine while drawing on Western concepts, it introduces a large number of original clinical formulas (including Sheng Xian Tang, Zhen Gan Xi Feng Tang, and Shen Zhe Zhen Qi Tang) and stands as a foundational text of the modern Chinese-Western medical-integration current.

description_zh: 近代张锡纯撰，分七期陆续刊行，成书年代约在民国七年（1918）至二十三年（1934）之间。全书包括医论、药物、方剂、医案、医话诸部分，见解独到、验方实用，为近代中医文献之重镇。
description_en: A major modern Chinese medical work by Zhang Xichun, issued in seven installments between roughly 1918 and 1934. Composed of medical discourses, materia medica, formulas, case records, and medical talks, it combines original theoretical insight with practically tested prescriptions, and stands as one of the most important Chinese medical texts of the Republican era.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_pinyin: Zá Bìng Zhèng Zhì Xīn Yì
title_en: New Meanings in the Pattern Identification and Treatment of Miscellaneous Diseases

significance.zh: 现代中医内科杂病辨治的代表作之一，融合古典理论与近现代临床经验。所创天麻钩藤饮为当代高血压、眩晕辨治的经典方，广泛见于当代方剂学教材。
significance.en: A representative modern work on pattern differentiation and treatment of miscellaneous internal medicine diseases, integrating classical TCM theory with early twentieth-century clinical experience. Its formulation Tian Ma Gou Teng Yin is now the canonical modern formula for hypertension and vertigo, included in most contemporary formulary textbooks.

description_zh: 胡光慈著，1956年成书。全书以内科杂病为纲，按证分类，详述病因病机、辨证要点与治法方药，是二十世纪中医内科杂病辨治研究的代表作之一。
description_en: Authored by Hu Guangci and completed in 1956. The text is organized by internal medicine miscellaneous diseases, classified by pattern, with detailed exposition of etiology, pathomechanism, differentiation essentials, and treatment formulas. It stands as one of the twentieth century's representative works on internal medicine miscellaneous disease management in Chinese medicine.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

title_en: Categorized Essentials of Orthopedics and Traumatology

significance.zh: 中医骨伤科早期专著之一。强调整体观念，主张内外兼治、气血为本，开创了以补益气血、调理脏腑治疗跌打损伤的思路，对后世骨伤科影响深远。
significance.en: One of the earliest monographs on Chinese orthopedics and traumatology. Emphasized the holistic approach and the primacy of qi and blood, pioneering the integrated internal-external treatment model in which tonifying qi-blood and regulating the viscera supports external manipulation of trauma. Profoundly influenced later orthopedic practice.

description_zh: 明代薛己著，1529年成书。分上、下两卷，上卷论跌打损伤、金创箭伤等伤科证治，下卷载所附验案。书中重视元气与脏腑功能对骨伤愈合的作用，确立补气养血、健脾理肝的治伤原则，为中医骨伤科奠基之作之一。
description_en: Written by Xue Ji of the Ming Dynasty, published in 1529. Divided into two volumes: the upper volume discusses pattern differentiation and treatment of traumatic injuries, wounds from weapons, and arrow wounds; the lower volume records case studies. The work emphasizes the role of the original qi and the viscera in wound healing, establishing the therapeutic principles of tonifying qi and nourishing blood, strengthening the spleen, and regulating the liver for trauma — principles foundational to the later development of Chinese orthopedics and traumatology.
